name: GraphQL __schema introspection enabled in production
description: Sends the standard `__schema { types { name } }` introspection query to /graphql. Vulnerable when the server returns a populated `types` array — every public schema field is now an attack-surface map for the caller. Most GraphQL frameworks ship with introspection ON by default; production deployments are expected to flip it off.

vulnerability.remediation: Disable introspection in production. HotChocolate: `.AddGraphQLServer().AddType<...>().ModifyOptions(o => o.DefaultBindingBehavior = BindingBehavior.Explicit)` and set introspection options per environment. Apollo Server: `introspection: false`. Strawberry: `introspection: false`. Validate via `curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"query":"{__schema{types{name}}}"}' <target>/graphql` — production should return a 200 with an `errors` array, not a populated `types` list.
